state_meaning.proposed: Package contracts and fixtures are available for review and local validation, but the package is not a stable default.
state_meaning.active: Package version is the current supported read capability version for consumers that accept this source.
state_meaning.suspected_broken: Recent test, post-check, site-shape, runtime evidence, or user report suggests the package may be invalid; consumers should show caution and prefer a known-good version when available.
state_meaning.broken: The package version failed validation or maintainer review and should not be admitted for new read runs except explicit diagnostic checks.
state_meaning.deprecated: The package version remains readable for historical run attribution or rollback display, but a newer replacement should be preferred.

lifecycle_state_vocabulary.owner_boundary: Lode owns lifecycle state and rollback facts. Core may use them for admission and run attribution. App may display them, but does not store package truth.

lifecycle.consumer_meaning: Package identity and contracts are available for review, fixture work, validator work, and repo-local Core fixture consumption checks. It is not a stable default execution package.

breaking_change_requires_new_version: input schema required fields or allowed origin constraints change
normalized output required fields or source/evidence ref policy changes
fixture source shape, redaction policy, normalized sample, or source/evidence placeholder binding changes
sample read package selection, fixture binding, or consumer entrypoint semantics change
Core consumption fixture resolution, schema validation case, or expected result changes
write deferred guardrail identity, deferred boundary, or admission blocking semantics change
post-check result contract or success conditions change
failure mapping category, outcome hint, or consumer boundary changes
local registry package path or manifest path changes
package lock ref, locked asset version, or invalidation behavior changes
non_breaking_change_policy: documentation wording can change without a version bump
planned asset refs can become present within the same proposed version when they preserve existing contracts
known limitations can become more precise without a version bump

rollback.consumer_boundary: Rollback candidates are package/version/lock refs only. They do not contain App install state, Core run truth, Harbor runtime state, raw evidence, credentials, or private local browser material.

lock_input.consumer_boundary: Lode supplies stable package, lock, asset version identity, a Core-readable fixture pointer, and a static write-deferred guardrail. App may choose an installed or pinned version later; Core records the referenced versions but does not infer hosted registry, marketplace, runtime, or write state from this file.

deprecation.recovery_hint: No replacement is declared for this proposed sample package.

revalidation_triggers: source shape or redaction policy changes
validator report fails after GH-96 validator coverage exists
Core fixture consumption fails after LODE-213
write deferred guardrail validation fails after LODE-213
invalidation.evidence_boundary: Invalidation notes may point to redacted fixtures, review notes, or external Core/Harbor evidence refs. They must not inline live evidence, raw payloads, runtime session state, credentials, or user business data.

failure_class_mapping.unsupported_version: Consumer requested a package or asset version that this package does not declare.
failure_class_mapping.deprecated: Consumer requested a package version whose lifecycle metadata marks it deprecated.
failure_class_mapping.invalid_contract: Lifecycle, version, lock, deprecation, or invalidation metadata is malformed or contradicts the manifest.
failure_class_mapping.resource_unavailable: Current Harbor facts cannot satisfy the package resource requirements.
failure_class_mapping.site_changed: The public source shape or required field mapping no longer matches the package fixture and post-check expectations.
failure_class_mapping.empty_result: A valid read produced no public content and should be displayed as an empty state.
failure_class_mapping.temporarily_invalid: Package is marked temporarily unavailable pending repair.
failure_class_mapping.verification_failed: A declared fixture, post-check, validator, or Core fixture consumption check failed.

non_goals: No App install, pin, update, rollback, or sync UI.
No hosted registry, marketplace, sync service, crawler queue, benchmark contract, or true write capability.
No live Core, Harbor, App, profile, provider, session, or evidence storage behavior.
